dialogue_text: I can still make her my slave, for she does not know how to use her power.
source_excerpt: The Wicked Witch was both surprised and worried when she saw the mark on Dorothy's forehead, for she knew well that neither the Winged Monkeys nor she, herself, dare hurt the girl in any way. She looked down at Dorothy's feet, and seeing...

dialogue_text: Come with me; and see that you mind everything I tell you, for if you do not I will make an end of you, as I did of the Tin Woodman and the Scarecrow.
source_excerpt: "Come with me; and see that you mind everything I tell you, for if you do not I will make an end of you, as I did of the Tin Woodman and the Scarecrow."

source_excerpt: "If I cannot harness you," said the Witch to the Lion, speaking through the bars of the gate, "I can starve you. You shall have nothing to eat until you do as I wish."

dialogue_text: I can starve you. You shall have nothing to eat until you do as I wish.
source_excerpt: "If I cannot harness you," said the Witch to the Lion, speaking through the bars of the gate, "I can starve you. You shall have nothing to eat until you do as I wish."

dialogue_text: Are you ready to be harnessed like a horse?
source_excerpt: So after that she took no food to the imprisoned Lion; but every day she came to the gate at noon and asked, "Are you ready to be harnessed like a horse?"

dialogue_text: No. If you come in this yard, I will bite you.
source_excerpt: And the Lion would answer, "No. If you come in this yard, I will bite you."
